Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to understand the normative process described

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Expected Flow When Inventory is Sufficient 1 Receive order 2 Check inventory must happen before payment fulfillment decisions 3 Authorize payment after confirming inventory 4 Package items after payment authorized 5 Ship order after items packaged 6 Close order final activity

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Expected Flow When Inventory is Insufficient 1 Receive order 2 Check inventory 3 Cancel order directly proceeds to cancel 4 Close order next reasonable step after cancel nothing else should follow

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key Constraints Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Close order should be the final activity in all scenarios

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order This follows the expected sufficiency flow perfectly No violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order Problem Package items occurs BEFORE Authorize payment This violates the rule that Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Violation identified

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order Multiple problems 1 Cancel order occurs after Authorize payment this suggests they knew inventory was insufficient but still authorized payment which does not make sense 2 Ship order occurs AFTER Cancel order this directly violates Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Major violations identified

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order This follows the expected insufficient inventory flow perfectly No violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order Problem Ship order occurs WITHOUT Package items in between This violates the rule that items must be packaged before they can be shipped Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Violation identified

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So my analysis Case 2 Violation Package items before Authorize payment Case 3 Violations Multiple issues including Cancel then Ship and authorization before cancellation Case 5 Violation Ship order without Package items Cases 1 and 4 are compliant with the normative process
